mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL prints persistent inflation that compresses equity multiples through higher real discount rates and squeezed margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months with above-median CPIAUCSL growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in UNRATE above 0.3 percentage points reflects deteriorating labor demand that reduces household income growth and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp in a single month.

mechanism_narrative: The product of month-over-month changes in RSAFS and HOUST captures demand coordination; a negative product reflects divergent consumer and housing trends that raise uncertainty premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when the product of RSAFS and HOUST month-over-month changes is negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, an elevated FEDFUNDS rate above 5% restricts credit availability and raises debt-service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in INDPRO reflects rising manufacturing utilization that boosts earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in PAYEMS and elevated FEDFUNDS above 4% generate a stagflationary drag where falling employment coincides with tight money, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when PAYEMS falls while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in HOUST below 1.5 million units reflects collapsing residential investment that drags down construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST falls below 1500 during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDPC1 growth below 2% annualized reflects demand stagnation that compresses corporate revenue growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 grows below 2% annualized.
